title: Polish responsive TUI cards and quota meters
summary: Polish the responsive quota arena, group shared routes without merging pools, and expose authoritative Nous purchased-credit remainder with explicit source freshness.
risks: Compact cards must still retain status, active selection, hottest quota meters, and reset facts
Variable row heights must not move mouse hit regions away from rendered cards
Wide and narrow terminals must fit without horizontal or vertical overflow
Exact-width ANSI lines must not gain an ellipsis
tests: Exact-width ANSI content pads without truncation or ellipsis
Card row heights follow meaningful content within minimum and maximum bounds
Row heights shrink to the terminal budget while preserving minimum card bodies
Incomplete grid rows are horizontally centered
Meter percentages remain fully visible at card width boundaries
Quota bars use a stable heavy-used and thin-remaining visual grammar
Active provider and model appear before quota and billing facts
Blocked cards show a COOLDOWN title and badge while unknown cards do not
Cooldown cards use a distinct panel treatment and appear as cooldown in the next-up strip
Claude falls back to an authenticated default profile when the configured active silo profile is signed out
Anonymous mode removes accounts, profiles, paths, referrals, bus content, and monetary details from frames
Loading screen lists each quota source with pending, done, and error state
Loading animation uses stable grouped ANSI runs and a lower redraw cadence
Harness cards sharing a canonical provider render adjacent with an explicit shared-route strip
Provider grouping does not merge accounts, percentages, resets, or availability evidence
Nous purchased_credits_remaining renders as a nonblocking paid-credit fact
Inactive Nous credit cache is labeled with age and never presented as live OpenAI quota
